params.name: Epitaph of Sikelos
params.description:
On Dec 7, 1883 AD, Sir Ramsay discovered a tombstone engraved with an epitaph in the town of Trelleis.
To commemorate his deceased wife, Sikelos engraved a lament on her tombstone. The overall meaning of the inscription is: "Enjoy life while you can, and don't complain when faced with adversity. Life is short, and fate is ever-changing." This tombstone, in the form of an ancient Greek musical notation, fully preserves the lyrics and rhythm of the entire lament. It is the most complete ancient …
